content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
428 J Street
• Sacramento, CA 958 12-0807
(916) 322-5660
Fax (916) 322-0886
December 28, 1999
Mister McAlister
Attorney at Law
13055 Orange Road
Wilton, California 95693-9664
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-99-293
Dear Mr. McAlister:
This letter responds to your request for advice about the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
I. QUESTION
May you transfer funds from your old campaign committee to a new committee for your
campaign for the Sacramento Superior Court in the year 2000 election?
II. CONCLUSION
Provided that all funds in the old account were raised prior to January 1, 1989 and have
not been commingled with subsequently raised campaign funds, you may transfer funds from the
old account to a new campaign committee for your upcoming campaign.
ilL FACTS
You served in the California State Assembly from 1971 through 1986. The balance in
your campaign committee account, ID 743431 (the “old account”), is $7,380.37, which was all
received prior to your departure from the Legislature. You plan to run for the Sacramento
Superior Court, Position #1, in the year 2000 election.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-99-293
Page 2
IV. ANALYSIS
Regulation 18519.4(d) covers the use of surplus campaign funds raised before
January 1, 1989, as were the funds in your old account. Among the permissible uses of such
funds, as prescribed in that regulation, is “[c]ontribution{] to any candidate, committee, or
political party, except where otherwise prohibited by law.” (Regulation 185 19.4(d)(3).)
Therefore, provided that they have not been commingled with funds raised on or after
January 1, 1989, you may transfer the funds remaining in your old account to a new committee
for your upcoming Superior Court campaign. (Section 89519; Regulation 185 19.4(d).)
I have enclosed a copy of Regulation 18519.4. Ifyou have any other questions regarding
this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca

Staff Counsel, Legal Division
